answer: The list spans two verses, Yj_1.4 and Yj_1.5: 'Manu, Atri, Viṣṇu, Hārīta, Yājñavalkya, Uśanas, Aṅgiras, Yama, Āpastamba, Saṃvarta, Kātyāyana, and Bṛhaspati, // Parāśara, Vyāsa, Śaṅkha, Likhita, Dakṣa, and Gautama, Śātātapa, and Vasiṣṭha are the promulgators of the Dharmaśāstras.' (Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch.1, paragraphs p-0f4ad0 and p-95ef53). The twenty named in order are: Manu, Atri, Viṣṇu, Hārīta, Yājñavalkya, Uśanas, Aṅgiras, Yama, Āpastamba, Saṃvarta, Kātyāyana, Bṛhaspati, Parāśara, Vyāsa, Śaṅkha, Likhita, Dakṣa, Gautama, Śātātapa, and Vasiṣṭha.

tool_calls.name: list_chapters
tool_calls.result_summary: Confirmed 3 chapters; ch.1 has translation variant

tool_calls.name: get_passage
tool_calls.result_summary: Yj_1.4 + Yj_1.5 list the twenty promulgators ending with Vasiṣṭha
